content.problemStatement:
"I have only one rule, never submit partially correct code" -Barney Stinson

The religious act which Barney and his friends hold most sacred, XORING the natural numbers in the given range. This time Barney is a bit busy with picking up some girls, so he asked you to help him. He gave you two numbers LLL and RRR, you have to find if XOR of all the numbers in range L to R (L,R both inclusive) is odd or even.

Warning!! Large Input-Output. Please use Fast IO.

###Input:


The first line will contain TTT, number of testcases.
Each testcase contains a single line of input, two integers L,RL, RL,R.
###Output:
For each testcase, in the new line print "Odd" if the XOR in the range is odd, else print "Even".

###Constraints


1≤T≤1061 \leq T \leq 10^61≤T≤106
1≤L≤R≤101 \leq L \leq  R \leq 101≤L≤R≤10181818
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers L and R, separated by a space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'Odd' if the XOR of all numbers in the range [L, R] is odd, and 'Even' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case, we calculate the XOR of all numbers in the given range and determine if it is odd or even.

solution.approach: To determine if the XOR of all numbers in the range [L, R] is odd or even, we can utilize the properties of XOR operations. Specifically, the XOR of any number with itself is 0 (even), and the XOR of any number with 0 is the number itself. Additionally, the XOR operation is associative and commutative. We can calculate the XOR of all numbers from 1 to R, and then XOR this result with the XOR of all numbers from 1 to L-1. This will give us the XOR of all numbers in the range [L, R]. If the result is odd, we print 'Odd'; otherwise, we print 'Even'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — each query is O(1), and we process T queries
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we use a constant amount of space to store the result
